hero.heading: Virtual Care
hero.subhead: When our office is closed and your care can't wait, we're still here — through Privia Virtual Clinic.

intro.heading: Still your VBAM
intro.body1: On Tuesday evenings and weekends, Vero Beach Adult Medicine provides on-call doctor coverage. When you call after hours, our team handles your concern in three steps: an administrative team member takes your message, an on-call nurse calls you back to assess, and if the nurse determines you need to speak with a doctor, a real VBAM physician will call — and if appropriate, see you virtually through Privia Virtual Clinic.
intro.body2: This isn't a referral to a stranger. It's your practice covering you when the office is closed.
intro.costNote: Virtual visits are billed separately and may have a co-pay. Have your insurance card handy. Call us with billing questions.

setup.heading: Two minutes of prep
setup.intro: The most common reason a virtual visit doesn't work is a camera or microphone blocked by your device. Here's how to clear it before you join.

mobile.label: On Your Phone
steps: Download the Privia Health app (App Store or Google Play), or open your visit link in your mobile browser.
Sign in to your Privia account before the visit starts.
When your phone asks for camera and microphone access, tap Allow.
Use Wi-Fi if possible — video calls are more stable than cellular.
mobile.permissionNote: Tap Allow — you'll see a prompt like this.

desktop.label: On Your Computer
steps: Use Chrome, Microsoft Edge, or Safari. Internet Explorer is not supported.
When your browser asks for camera and microphone access, click Allow.
If you see a camera icon with a line through it in the address bar, click it to change the permission to Allow.
Close Zoom, FaceTime, Teams, or any other app currently using your camera.
desktop.permissionNote: Click Allow — you'll see a prompt like this.

items.label: Strong connection
items.body: Wi-Fi is more reliable than cellular for video. If the call is choppy, move closer to your router.

items.label: Good lighting
items.body: Face a window or a lamp so the provider can see you clearly. Avoid sitting with a bright light behind you.

items.label: Insurance & medications
items.body: Have your insurance card and a current medication list nearby before you join.

items.label: Join a few minutes early
items.body: Connecting early gives you time to resolve any setup issues before your visit starts.

items.label: Stay charged
items.body: Plug in your device or confirm your battery is above 20% before the visit begins.

helpCta.heading: We're a phone call
helpCta.body: If you run into technical difficulties or have questions before your virtual visit, call our office — we'll help you get connected.
